title: USYSSTAT
text: USYSSTAT USYSSTAT is a Uniface component that provides operations for managing component state in a temporary table. It can be used as-is, or as a starting point or template for creating your own state management mechanism. Description The component source is available as usysstat.xml in the /uniface/misc directory of the Uniface installation. The definitions of the USTATE table are contained in the umeta.xml file. This enables you to customize the state management mechanism, or use it as an example for creating your own mechanism. The USYSSTAT component stores the following state information into the temporary table USTATE: uSessionId (PK)—session ID; it can be obtained by retrieving the value of SESSION in $webinfo("webservercontext") . uInstanceName (PK)—instance ID, you can get it using $instancename . UComponentVariables —GOLD-separated list of packed component variables. Operations SetState —writes information to the database table USTATE. GetState —retrieves information to the database table USTATE. DeleteState —deletes state information of a session. CleanUpState —for bulk maintenance to clean up outdated records in USTATE. Related Links Uniface Temporary Tables for State Management Uniface provides a temporary table mechanism for automatically handling state. This approach is suitable for maintaining large amount of data. Using Default Temporary Tables to Maintain State Uniface provides an automatic temporary-table mechanism for you to maintain state. It makes use of the default ProcScript supplied in the preActivate and postActivate triggers. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
